title: Photo Content Analysis — semantic layer for the AI Director (Phase A / node 2)
description: Contract for analysis/photo_content.json. It merges the rule-based technical signals from analysis/photos.json (quality, orientation, focal point) with a SEMANTIC layer (what is in the photo + narrative scores). The semantic layer is produced by one batched vision call; see $defs/aiResult for exactly what the vision model may return. Guardrail: the model returns records keyed by INDEX only and never emits file paths, config, or any field outside $defs/aiResult — the scaffold owns the index->file mapping and clamps/validates every field.

generatedBy.description: 'stub' while the vision call is not wired, else 'vision:<model-id>'. Downstream/QA reads this to know whether the semantic fields are real or placeholder.

model.description: Vision model id when generatedBy != stub.

tag.description: Controlled wedding vocabulary (whitelist — like the effect enum). The model must pick from this list; unknown tags are dropped by the scaffold.

aiResult.description: EXACTLY what the vision model returns per photo. No file paths, no config — keyed by index into the batch the scaffold sent. This is also the validation contract the scaffold enforces on the model's raw output before merging.

index.description: 0-based position in the batch (scaffold maps it back to a file).

heroScore.description: How much this photo deserves a hero / full-bleed slot.

storyImportance.description: How load-bearing this photo is for the narrative.

photoRecord.description: One merged record: technical fields carried from photos.json + the semantic fields from aiResult.

file.description: Path, owned by the scaffold (never by the model).

focusX.description: Carried focal point for face-safe cropping.

source.description: Per-record provenance of the semantic fields.
